_doc: Bibliography / source registry. Every `source` id used across the SSOT must resolve here. Figures are sources too (partOf their publication).

site-surface-state.title: Present-day surface treatment of the excavated shafts (archaeological park)
site-surface-state.note: Present-day surface state of the shaft heads at the Raschpëtzer archaeological park: the excavated shafts are covered with modern metal lids; P5 and P-4 carry windowed viewing covers; P4 keeps its documented concrete slab cap. Corroborated by public site descriptions (Wikipedia / Subterranea Britannica / Visit Luxembourg) and Brochure 2018.

brochure2018.title: The Raschpëtzer — A Roman Underground Water Supply System
brochure2018.publisher: Syndicat d'initiative et de tourisme de la Commune de Walferdange
brochure2018.rights: © the authors / SIT Walferdange; figures reused for research/education
brochure2018.note: Primary source; cited by page [p.N] and figure [fig.N-M].

geoportail_lu.title: Luxembourg national orthophoto (RGB), 2019 edition
authors: Administration du cadastre et de la topographie (ACT)
geoportail_lu.note: Aerial base-map imagery draped for spatial context; INSPIRE WMS layer OI_OrthoimageCoverage_RGB_2019. Matches the 2019 ACT LiDAR terrain epoch.

fig-2-1.title: Roman settlements, catchment areas and the explored qanat conduit

fig-3-1.title: 3D model of the explored qanat conduit (P-7A to P9)

fig-4-2.title: Simplified geological cross-section through the catchment area

fig-4-3.title: Geological structure of the Pëtschend horst (section XY), strata legend

fig-3-1b.title: 3D block model P-7A→P9 with a 100 m scale bar
fig-3-1b.note: The only figure showing all shaft positions along the route at a stated scale (100 m bar). Oblique 3D perspective — gives approximate along-route spacing, NOT georeferenced coordinates. Source of the schematic modelPos arrangement.

fig-5-3.title: Schematic longitudinal section (initial planning)
fig-5-3.note: Slopes 'very exaggerated for reasons of clarity' — schematic, not to scale.

fig-5-4.title: Photo of shaft P2 (backfilled, in original state)
fig-5-4.note: Corrected: fig 5-4 is a PHOTOGRAPH of P2, not a route/spacing plan. It was previously mis-cited as the source of modelPos spacing; the actual arrangement source is fig-3-1b.

fig-5-11.title: P-4 overflow weir and lateral channel (plan + cross sections)

fig-5-24.title: Gallery near P7 with channel; floor at sandstone/marl boundary

fig-6-7.title: View into the 36 m deep shaft P5

survey-shaft-floors.locator: shaft detail drawings P-7A / P-5A / P-4
survey-shaft-floors.title: Surveyed shaft-floor elevations (m a.s.l.)
survey-shaft-floors.note: Source of REAL_FLOOR elevations P-7A 354.3, P-5A 357.5, P-4 355.4 m.

osm-qanat-shafts.title: Raschpëtzer qanat shaft nodes (historic=archaeological_site) in OpenStreetMap
osm-qanat-shafts.note: 14 shaft nodes 'Quanat -6..9' retrieved by Overpass query nwr(around:1200,49.668,6.139)[name~aschp|anat|ëtsch|aedch]. Community-mapped, not certified survey-grade; see data/osm-shafts.json for the raw import, node IDs and caveats.

eudem-opentopodata.title: EU-DEM v1.1 (25 m) sampled through the OpenTopoData API
eudem-opentopodata.license: EU-DEM / Copernicus terms; OpenTopoData public instance
eudem-opentopodata.note: Source of the current GeoData terrain grid (geodata-walferdange.js); 4x-area bbox 6.106–6.186 E, 49.656–49.680 N on a 96x40 grid. NOTE (2026-07-02): at 25 m it is systematically +10 to +21 m too high over the qanat corridor and misses the Haedchen depression; superseded for the corridor by act-lidar-2019 (below). Retained for now as broad landscape context.

act-lidar-2019.title: ACT LiDAR 2019 — Modèle numérique de terrain (0.5 m), Luxembourg
act-lidar-2019.license: CC0 / open data (Administration du cadastre et de la topographie)
act-lidar-2019.attribution: © Administration du cadastre et de la topographie (ACT), Luxembourg
act-lidar-2019.sampling: Point queries to map.geoportail.lu/raster?lon=&lat= (layer 'dhm'), one HTTP call per point, coords transformed WGS84→EPSG:2169 (pyproj). No bulk/WCS download (the full DTM is a 27 GB national mosaic).
act-lidar-2019.resolution: Source raster 0.5 m. The baked terrain grid (geodata-walferdange.js) is sampled on a 40×22 lattice over 6.138–6.162E / 49.6618–49.6708N ⇒ ~44 m (E–W) × ~48 m (N–S) node spacing — the mesh resolution, coarser than the source. Per-shaft surfaceElevM are exact 0.5 m point queries at the OSM coordinates. 0 voids over the area; sampled range 231.7–405.1 m.
act-lidar-2019.note: 0.5 m LiDAR-derived bare-earth DTM. Sampled pointwise at the OSM shaft coordinates it matches the brochure's floor+depth surfaces to 0.5–2.5 m (vs EU-DEM's +10–21 m) and resolves the Haedchen depression — externally validating the documented depths and the near-level gallery. Highest-credibility surface source for the corridor.

werner-sit-survey.title: Coordonnées Raschpëtzer — surveyed shaft coordinates (SIT Walferdange), LUREF (EPSG:2169)
werner-sit-survey.note: Unpublished survey table (PDF: 'coordonnées Raschpetzer') plus CAD site plans (DXF/DWG), provided directly to this project by Henri Werner — a co-author of brochure2018 and one of the site's excavators/surveyors. Lists LUREF (EPSG:2169) X/Y and, for most points, a ground-surface Z (m a.s.l.) for the shaft CENTREPOINT ('Petz Mittelpunkt' — the well/shaft-mouth centre), for the main line P-1 through P9. P-1 and P0 are marked '~' (approximate marking, not a centrepoint survey); P2/P3 are 'Mittelpunkt Mulde' (centre of the surface depression, both still backfilled). Does NOT cover the western/auxiliary shafts P-4/P-5/P-5A/P-7A, which remain OSM-derived (see osm-qanat-shafts). Supersedes the OSM/similarity-fit reconciliation (pd-osm-georef) for the 11 covered shafts — the correction is largest at P2 (10.9 m), matching that reconciliation's own finding that the OSM 'P2' node read materially too far east.
